title: eleocharis palustris subsp. mamillata
intro: Eleocharis palustris subsp. mamillata, also known as the small spike-rush, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sedge family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Eleocharis palustris subsp. mamillata include treating digestive issues, reducing menstrual cramps, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is traditionally prepared as an herbal tea or tincture by drying the roots and leaves, and steeping them in hot water or a solvent to extract its medicinal properties.
